growth_records.strain: Reactors RP1/RP2 (propionate-fed enrichment); MAG4 = 'Ca. Thermosyntrophopropionicum ammoniitolerans'
growth_records.medium: Bicarbonate-buffered basal medium (BM, ref 17) + ammonium chloride + sodium propionate

up.units: community-level; reactor fed 0.1 M (9.6 g/L) sodium propionate at HRT 28 d, propionate degraded to fluctuating residual 0.8-3.9 g/L (RP2) / stable low (RP1)
up.method: reported (volumetric, community; per-gDW specific rate not derivable - no biomass concentration given)

sec.units: mol CH4 per mol propionate mineralised (biogas 62-70% CH4)
transient intermediate accumulating during propionate oxidation (RP2 fluctuated up to 3.5 g/L; maintained <2.5 g/L when stable)
maintained at low partial pressure 3.5-12 Pa (up to 4-30 Pa)

growth_records.cite: Singh et al. Syntrophic entanglements for propionate and acetate oxidation under thermophilic and high-ammonia conditions. The ISME Journal (2023). DOI:10.1038/s41396-023-01504-y. [PMC10579422]
growth_records.snippet: Four identical laboratory-scale continuously stirred (80 rpm) tank reactors ... working volume 1.1 L were operated at 52 °C ... fed ... at a dilution rate of 26 µL min−1, resulting in a hydraulic retention time of 28 days ... supplemented w
growth_records.notes: Continuous CSTR, no biomass retention => at steady state specific growth rate mu approx D. D = 1/HRT = 1/28 day = 0.0357 day-1 = 0.0357/24 = 0.00149 h-1. Doubling time td = ln2/D = 0.693/0.0357 = 19.4 day = 466 h (~465 h). CH4 yield stated verbatim: '1.75 moles of methane were generated for each mole of propionate mineralised'. All rates are COMMUNITY-LEVEL/volumetric (mixed syntrophic enrichment, uncultured MAG candidates); no biomass (gDW) concentration reported, so per-organism specific uptake/secretion fluxes (mmol/gDW/h) are NOT derivable. Full BM composition given only by reference [17], hence medium_composition_stated=false. pH 8.1-8.3 in propionate reactors (recorded as 8.2). Species-specific doubling times were estimated from specific methane production rate (td=ln2/mu_CH4) but the numeric mu/td values reside in Supplementary tables not present in the article XML.

growth_records.strain: Reactors RA1/RA2 (acetate-fed enrichment); MAG9 = novel thermophilic SAOB candidate
growth_records.medium: Bicarbonate-buffered basal medium (BM, ref 17) + ammonium chloride + sodium acetate

up.units: community-level; reactor fed 0.1 M (8.2 g/L) sodium acetate at HRT 28 d, acetate maintained stable at ~0.7-0.9 g/L
up.method: reported (volumetric, community; per-gDW specific rate not derivable - no biomass concentration given)

sec.units: biogas 62-70% CH4 (syntrophic acetate oxidation -> H2/CO2 -> CH4 via hydrogenotrophic methanogens)
low partial pressure maintained (H2/formate interspecies transfer)

growth_records.cite: Singh et al. Syntrophic entanglements for propionate and acetate oxidation under thermophilic and high-ammonia conditions. The ISME Journal (2023). DOI:10.1038/s41396-023-01504-y. [PMC10579422]
growth_records.snippet: acetate content remained stable at around 0.7–0.9 g L−1 in the acetate-fed reactors RA1 and RA2 ... supplemented with 8.9 g L−1 ammonium chloride (3 g L−1 NH4+-N) and 0.1 M (8.2 g L−1) sodium acetate (RA1, RA2).
growth_records.notes: Continuous CSTR, steady state mu approx D. D = 1/HRT = 1/28 day = 0.0357 day-1 = 0.00149 h-1; td = ln2/D = 19.4 day = ~465 h. Acetate degraded via syntrophic acetate oxidation (SAOB) coupled to hydrogenotrophic methanogenesis; unlike propionate, acetate degradation did NOT depend critically on Methanothermobacter. Rates are community-level/volumetric; no biomass (gDW) reported so specific fluxes (mmol/gDW/h) not derivable. Full BM composition by reference [17] only => medium_composition_stated=false.

growth_records.medium: Undiluted reactor culture (RP1) in anaerobic serum bottle, spiked with sodium propionate

up.units: g propionate/L/day (batch cultivation, volumetric, community; up to; added propionate degraded within 25 days in RP1 batches)
up.method: reported (volumetric; per-gDW specific rate not derivable - no biomass given)

sec.units: transient intermediate accumulating to 2-3 g/L before SAOB initiated acetate degradation
produced concomitant with propionate/acetate degradation; ~1.75 mol CH4 per mol propionate

growth_records.cite: Singh et al. Syntrophic entanglements for propionate and acetate oxidation under thermophilic and high-ammonia conditions. The ISME Journal (2023). DOI:10.1038/s41396-023-01504-y. [PMC10579422]
growth_records.snippet: Batch cultivation demonstrated degradation rates of up to 0.16 g propionate L−1 day−1 at hydrogen partial pressure 4–30 Pa ... The communities originating from RP1 (batches B01-B02) had the highest propionate-degradation rate and degraded t
growth_records.notes: Batch, 52 C, pH 8.5 +/- 0.1, free ammonia 1.2 g NH3/L. Reported volumetric propionate degradation rate up to 0.16 g propionate L-1 day-1 (community-level). Rapid RP1 propionate degradation produced 2-3 g acetate/L transiently before syntrophic acetate oxidation began. H2 3.5-12 Pa (Table S5); abstract states 4-30 Pa. CH4 yield 1.75 mol/mol propionate (verbatim). Mixed uncultured enrichment => no biomass (gDW), no per-organism specific flux (mmol/gDW/h) derivable; propionate spike target ~30 mM (actual varied). Full medium composition not stated (undiluted reactor culture); medium_composition_stated=false.
